summary: Fabric v2.0 clean rebrand to 'knowledge sustainment protocol' positioning. Implements 4 MCP tools (2 existing + 2 new: fab_extract_knowledge, fab_review), 3 Skills (fabric-archive/review/import) with intent-inference design, Stop hooks across all three clients (Claude Code/Cursor/Codex), new dual-root directory layout (personal at ~/.fabric, team at <repo>/.fabric), path-decoupled stable_id (KP-/KT- prefix + monotonic counter), 5-type/3-maturity schema, doctor --lint with 6 deterministic checks. Released as 4 RCs over ~5.5 weeks. Clean-slate migration: existing .fabric/rules/ deleted (no users yet).

implementation_scope.objective: rc.1 — Clean rebrand foundation: directory layout, schema, stable_id mechanism, init-time deterministic scan
implementation_scope.rationale: v2.0 establishes new architectural primitives (dual-root, path-decoupled stable_id, type/maturity/layer schema) and clears legacy. Delete-not-archive per user clean-slate preference; no users = no migration. Init-time scan produces 4-7 baseline entries so post-init knowledge base is non-empty.
acceptance_criteria: Old .fabric/rules/ + INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md + bootstrap/ deleted (no .fabric-v1-archive/)
New .fabric/knowledge/{decisions,pitfalls,guidelines,models,processes}/ + pending/ structure created by fabric init
~/.fabric/knowledge/ auto-created on first personal write
Frontmatter schema includes id (KP-/KT- + type code + monotonic counter), type (5 enum), maturity (3 enum), layer (2 enum), layer_reason, created_at
stable_id counter persisted in agents.meta.json; file git mv does NOT regenerate id
Init-time deterministic scan produces 4-7 baseline entries (tech stack, module structure, build config, code style, CI, README first paragraph)
Existing fab_plan_context + fab_get_rule_sections work against new schema
doctor existing checks adapted to new schema (no false positives on new layout)
fabric init runs clean on empty repo and creates v2.0 layout
implementation_scope.change_summary: Replace v1.x .fabric/rules/ scaffolding with v2.0 .fabric/knowledge/ structure. Update all schemas (frontmatter parser, agents.meta.json, event ledger types). Add init-time deterministic scan as new CLI subcommand internal to init. Adapt existing two MCP tools to new data shape.

implementation_scope.objective: rc.2 — Archive loop: fab_extract_knowledge MCP + fabric-archive skill + Stop hooks across three clients
implementation_scope.rationale: Closes the WRITE half of the knowledge cycle. Skill internalizes prompt template (no separate MCP tool). Hooks provide reminder layer across all three clients with cross-client validated support (Cursor: cursor.com/cn/docs/hooks; Codex: developers.openai.com/codex/hooks).
acceptance_criteria: fab_extract_knowledge MCP tool registered with schema validation
fab_extract_knowledge writes to pending/ with knowledge.proposed event (or knowledge.archive_attempted on empty)
Idempotency: same source_session repeat call doesn't duplicate; appends evidence to existing entries
fabric-archive skill SKILL.md contains: layer classification heuristic (strong team / strong personal / default team), 5-type extraction prompt template, decision tree for 'is this worth archiving'
Skill installed at <repo>/.claude/skills/fabric-archive/ AND <repo>/.codex/skills/fabric-archive/ (Cursor reads either)
CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules each get a one-line pointer to fabric-archive skill
Stop hook script at .fabric/hooks/archive-hint.js (Node, cross-platform) reads events.jsonl and decides reminder
Three client hook configs install during fabric init: .claude/settings.json (CC), .cursor/hooks.json (Cursor), Codex equivalent
Hook threshold: 5 plan_contexts since last knowledge.proposed OR 24h, whichever first
Dogfood test: in Fabric self repo, manually run archive flow → pending entries created → events.jsonl trail complete
implementation_scope.change_summary: Implement archive write-side: new MCP tool, new skill template, hook script + 3 client configs. Update fabric init to install all of above.

implementation_scope.objective: rc.3 — Review loop: fab_review MCP + fabric-review skill with mode inference + pending promotion
implementation_scope.rationale: Closes the APPROVAL half. Mode inference (pending/topic/health/revisit) replaces user-facing mode-picker — system infers from invocation context. Layer flip is the only legal stable_id mutation (KT ↔ KP), with knowledge.layer_changed event for audit.
acceptance_criteria: fab_review MCP tool with action enum: list / approve / reject / modify / search / defer
approve action accepts batch ids (single + array)
approve performs git mv from pending/ to {personal|team}/{type}/, writes knowledge.promoted event
modify action handles layer flip: regenerate id (KT-X-N → KP-X-M or vice versa), write knowledge.layer_changed event
search action supports topic/type/tag/date filters

fabric-review skill SKILL.md contains: mode inference rules (user message scan → events.jsonl recent → pending count), per-mode flow, semantic check guidance for [b] mode (LLM-assisted duplicate/contradiction detection)
Skill DOES NOT use AskUserQuestion to ask 'what mode' — system infers; AskUserQuestion only for genuine choices (approve/reject/modify per item)
Skill installed at .claude/skills/fabric-review/ AND .codex/skills/fabric-review/
Hook archive-hint.js extended: second signal for pending overflow (count >= 10 OR age >= 7 days) → recommends fabric-review skill
Filesystem-edit fallback: if user manually moves file from pending/ to {personal|team}/, doctor identifies it on next run and writes knowledge.promoted event
Dogfood test: review the rc.2 pending entries end-to-end; at least one layer flip and one rejection in trail
implementation_scope.change_summary: Implement review approve-side with mode inference. Extend Hook script with second signal (pending overflow). Add filesystem-edit fallback path.

implementation_scope.objective: rc.4 — Lint + import + README + docs: complete v2.0 surface
implementation_scope.rationale: Lifecycle/lint is the moat per Anthropic/Letta evidence. fabric-import LLM-driven enrichment uses all v2.0 infrastructure for cold-start projects. README reframe corrects positioning narrative.
acceptance_criteria: doctor --lint implements 6 deterministic checks: orphan demote (90/30/14 day thresholds), stale archive, stable_id duplicate, layer mismatch (KT in personal/), index drift, pending overdue (>14 days)
doctor --apply-lint writes knowledge.demoted / knowledge.archived events
doctor --lint default behavior: report only, no file mutation
fabric-import skill SKILL.md contains: 3-phase pipeline (P1 codebase profile internalized to init scan / P2 git+doc mining / P3 dedup against existing), .fabric/.import-state.json checkpoint logic
fabric-import skill default layer: team (project artifacts are team-level)
README rewritten with B' tagline ('Fabric — cross-client knowledge for AI agents.'), pain-point opening, ASCII architecture diagram in first 100 words
README has 'Why Fabric' section explaining metaphor evolution from AGENTS.md era to knowledge-sustainment era
docs/knowledge-types.md created with 5-type semantic definitions, examples, decision criteria
docs/initialization.md updated for v2.0 flow
docs/roadmap.md adds v2.0 + v2.1 (team-knowledge.git + 3-role permissions) + v2.x (semantic search, federated teams)
CHANGELOG documents all RC milestones
v2.0.0 stable release (no -rc suffix) tagged after dogfood validation passes
implementation_scope.change_summary: Implement lint, LLM-driven import skill, and complete documentation. Final RC also includes v2.0.0 stable release.

code_anchors.snippet: currently registers 2 MCP tools: fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections
code_anchors.significance: Where 2 new MCP tools (fab_extract_knowledge, fab_review) get registered in rc.2/rc.3

code_anchors.snippet: hand-rolled regex frontmatter parser (flat scalars + flow-style arrays)
code_anchors.significance: Constrains schema design to flat scalars (no nested maturity object). v2.0 fields: id/type/maturity/layer/layer_reason/created_at all flat.
rc.1 must change this to path-decoupled (counter-based KP-/KT- prefix). Only legal id mutation: review-time layer flip.
rc.4 doctor --lint extends this with 6 new checks following same pattern

code_anchors.snippet: buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown — currently builds structural L0/L1/L2 topology, NOT 5-type taxonomy
code_anchors.significance: rc.1 deletes this function entirely; INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md is replaced by docs/knowledge-types.md as human reference

code_anchors.snippet: two-call workflow (fab_plan_context returns selection_token, then fab_get_rule_sections)
code_anchors.significance: Existing L0/L1/L2 protocol stays as the consumption pattern; rc.1 only adapts data shape

key_findings: 5-layer storage tree refuted as moat (Karpathy gist is 3 layers; no peer team published 5-layer; industry uses 2-tier + nested AGENTS.md)
INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md does NOT cover article's 5 types — it is structural L0/L1/L2 topology only
Fabric L0/L1/L2 ≠ article 3-tier index (different axes: rule precedence vs progressive disclosure)
Lifecycle/decay/lint is the most defensible differentiator (Anthropic + Letta evidence)
stable_id is path-derived in v1.x → directory reorganization breaks ledger replay
Cross-client MCP is genuine whitespace (no direct competitor positioned as 'MCP-first cross-client knowledge protocol')
AI delivery teams as headline = OSS adoption inversion (dbt/Airflow/Backstage all bottom-up first)
Karpathy's actual gist scope = 3 ops + 3-layer; article extrapolates the team-engineering use case
No current users → migration tax is zero → v2.0 clean rebrand strictly better than v1.x staged
Cursor reads .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/ — no separate Cursor skill path needed
Hook layer is fully cross-client (Claude Code Stop + Cursor stop + Codex hooks all confirmed)
fab_extract_knowledge can be cross-client because it's MCP-tool-based (no Skill dependency); Skill provides prompt template
Skill is unified container for prompt template + decision tree + workflow; eliminates need for separate fab_get_extraction_prompt MCP
review mode should be inferred from context (recent events, user message, pending count), not asked via AskUserQuestion
Layer flip is the only legal stable_id mutation; everything else is id-stable across moves
doctor --lint should split into 6 deterministic checks (Fabric core) + 2 semantic checks (fabric-review skill, LLM-assisted)
Decay thresholds 90/30/14 days are 1/4 of article's 12/6/N months — reflects single-repo low-frequency use
fabric-import deterministic init-scan (rc.1) + LLM-driven enrichment skill (rc.4) split is correct architectural decomposition
decision_context: Boundary B (data + lifecycle + async-review primitive): chosen over A (data only, too thin) and C (full platform, wrong fit)
v2.0 clean rebrand chosen over v1.x staged: triggered by user 'no current users / migration acceptable' input
fab_extract_knowledge always writes to pending/ (no target param): keeps responsibilities pure (extract != promote)
fab_review action enum: list/approve/reject/modify/search/defer — single MCP tool serves all review actions to stay under Cursor 40-tool cap
3 Skills (archive/review/import), not 4+ : prompt templates internalized, no separate fab_get_extraction_prompt
Stop hook is reminder layer (exit 2 + stderr/followup_message), not control layer; never blocks Agent permanently
Dual root (~/.fabric + repo/.fabric) over single root: gitignore can't filter by frontmatter; personal must be physically separate
stable_id = KP-/KT- prefix + type code + monotonic counter: human-readable, path-decoupled, monotonic
Layer flip is the only legal stable_id mutation: review-time user-explicit action with knowledge.layer_changed audit event
Mode inference for fabric-review skill: AskUserQuestion is for genuine choices, not for what system can deduce
doctor --lint default = report only; --apply-lint required for actual mutation: audit safety > convenience
Cursor & Codex CLI hook support confirmed (Round 5): Hook layer is fully cross-client
Keep 'Fabric' name + redefine metaphor: rebranding cost real even with no users; SEO won't immediately improve with new name
Delete .fabric/rules/ directly (not archive): per user clean-slate preference, recorded to long-term memory
fabric-import in v2.0 (not v2.1): split deterministic part to rc.1 init-scan + LLM-driven part to rc.4 skill
